title: Surrounded Regions
prompt: Given an m x n board of 'X' and 'O', capture all regions of 'O' that are completely surrounded by 'X' (flip those to 'X'). 'O's connected to the border are not captured. Modify in-place and return the board.
hints: Reverse the question: 'O's that are NOT captured are exactly those connected (4-directionally) to a border 'O'.
Mark all border-connected 'O's (e.g., temporarily as 'S'), then sweep: 'O' → 'X', 'S' → 'O'.

optimal.approach: DFS/BFS from border 'O's marking safe cells.

pitfalls: DFS recursion depth on max-size boards (200×200) can blow the stack — use iterative or increase stack size.
Forgetting that diagonal connectivity does NOT count.
